evidence.scientific_clarity: Dual-axis log-loss + cosine LR plot directly satisfies the brief; color-coupled axis labels aid reading; both trends are clearly visible.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 set; PDF embeds DejaVu Sans as Type3 (non-editable outlines); svg.fonttype defaults to 'path' (also outlines); DejaVu Sans is the matplotlib default, not a deliberate choice.
evidence.palette: Blue/red pair is not the worst for colorblind viewers (distinguishable under deuteranopia) but red is conventionally reserved for 'bad/high' direction which conflicts with LR decay; no explicit colorblind-safe palette (e.g. Wong/Okabe-Ito) used.
evidence.layout_density: tight_layout applied, 7×4 inch canvas well-used, legend placed upper-right without overlap, lr y-axis anchored at 0, no gaping whitespace.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption correctly describes both axes and their directional semantics; legend present; however no specific numerical claim (e.g. 'loss falls from X to Y') and the legend duplicates axis-label information without adding value.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf exists but fonts are Type3 (non-selectable/non-editable); script never sets matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42; text cannot be selected or edited in Illustrator/Inkscape.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (17 KB), figure.png (74 KB, 1050×600), figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains dual-axis layout, log scale, unit scaling, and directional asymmetry of 'better'; missing a concrete numerical finding (e.g. final loss value or LR range) that would anchor the reader.

overall_strengths: Color-coupled axis tick labels and line colors cleanly implement the brief's hint and aid dual-axis readability.
All required output files are present and non-trivial; tight_layout and sensible axis limits produce a clean, publication-ready layout.
overall_weaknesses: PDF uses Type3 (bitmap-at-size) font embedding instead of TrueType (fonttype=42), making text non-selectable and non-editable in vector editors.

No specific numerical claim in the caption and no explicit colorblind-safe palette; DejaVu Sans default font was never overridden to a more professional choice (e.g. Helvetica, Source Sans, or a serif).
